entry.title: Re: Getting Started with Joule Studio in SAP Build - Part 1: Provisioning Joule Studio

Informative blog Diganta, Do we have some information for Customer Setup what type of subaccounts would be required for e.g. Dev, Test and Prod  for Joule studio and Integration with CI/CD for deployment or Integration with Cloud transport for transports between subaccounts?

Best Regards,

Manas Behra

entry.title: Parent-Child Drill-Down Hierarchy from S/4 CDS View to SAC Live Connection
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to develop a Parent-Child drill-down hierarchy in an SAP S/4HANA CDS View and then consume this hierarchy in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using a Live Connection.

I have already tried creating the hierarchy on the CDS side, and the hierarchy is visible in SAC, but it does not behave like a proper drill-down hierarchy when consumed through the live connection.

Could anyone please confirm:

  • Is it possible to create a Parent-Child drill-down hierarchy completely on the CDS side and consume it in SAC via Live Connection?
  • Are there any specific annotations, hierarchy definitions, or SAC limitations that need to be considered?
  • Has anyone successfully implemented this scenario?

Any guidance or sample approach would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Agentic AI in Practice: Concepts, Architecture, and Your First LangGraph Agent (Part 1 of 6)
entry.content: @DEEPA_DORAIRAJ   - also this does not look like a Langgraphs agent where you define nodes and edges. Can you please check. This is a react agent.

entry.title: Re: Temporary Time Information not triggering a Data Repl...
entry.content:
SAP has finally released a KBA in relation to this:

3753931 - Error : "you cannot change an individual work schedule that has already been assigned as a temporary work schedule" - SAP for Me

Symptom

Below error is occured while editing an individual work schedule. 

"You cannot change an individual work schedule that has already been assigned as a temporary work schedule". 

Environment

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Time Management

Reproducing the Issue

  1. Go to Administer Time for a user.
  2. Open a Temporary Time Information record for a specific date.
  3. Attempt to modify the Individual Work Schedule fields (for example, change the duration).
  4. Save the change.
  5. Observe the error: "You cannot change an individual work schedule that has already been assigned as a temporary work schedule."
  6. This error is also occurred while modifying the individual work schedule from Manage data. 

Cause

  • When a work schedule is assigned in Job Information, there are existing validations that detect this linkage and the work schedule cannot be modified. However, this validation did not previously apply to Individual Work Schedules that are assigned to the Temporary Time Information.
  • Starting with the 1H 2026 release, this gap is addressed. The system will introduce a validation for Individual Work Schedules assigned in Temporary Time Information.

Resolution

  1. Delete the existing Temporary Time Information record that contains the Individual Work Schedule you intended to modify.
  2. Once done, you can modify the individual work schedule.

entry.title: Re: RAP Event: consume SAP standard event and trigger custom RAP event

entry.title: Re: New POD 2.0 Framework: Tips and Tricks

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/application-help-for-manage-pods-2-0/pod-plugin-migration-guide

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Custom notifications and widget events in SAP DM POD 2.0
entry.content: Hi! If my answer was helpful, please consider accepting it as solution.

entry.title: Re: Considerations when implementing IAS for SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
@NathanWilkinson 
Great point — in a mature setup, onboarding completion prior to start date would be enforced. However, in our industry that’s just not realistic. It often only becomes a priority when payroll deadlines approach and access to timesheeting (UKG) is needed.

So while I agree this is ultimately an HR/process challenge, we’re dealing with the downstream impact.

On the AD side:

  • Provisioning is legacy and sits outside SuccessFactors
  • Managed manually by IT (yes — via forms from managers…)
  • Once provisioned and the start date hits, the business email writes back to SF and therefore the IAS profile (IT won’t delay this)

This creates the IAS conflict when onboarding isn’t complete. Given that, I’m not trying to redesign the process, just looking for practical controls to reduce:

  • Manual IAS intervention
  • Repeated email resets and candidate troubleshooting

Pre-IAS, our onboarding SMEs could handle password resets and restarts themselves. Now with IAS, we can’t reasonably give them that level of access (e.g. deleting accounts, resetting credentials).

You mentioned your customers’ onboarding teams have IAS access — is this full admin, or a more restricted model?

Ideally, this should sit with Tier 1 support. Right now it’s requiring specialist intervention, which doesn’t feel appropriate for what is essentially candidate support.

Thanks as well for the insight around job timing and sequencing — that’s really helpful context. We’ve just approved our implementation partner to review and update our job schedule

entry.title: Re: scope items missing for EWM best practice for S/4 han...
entry.content:
Hi,  It's not clear what's question.

Please check following following process information:
https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2025-FPS0/?region=DE&share=c93b3ee9-610c-4924-9fae-bf5d3fca6623

Best regards,
Chris

entry.title: SAP HANA Database Explorer – Unable to paste values into table cells
entry.content:
Hi support team,

We are no longer able to paste values into table cells.

Environment
Database Explorer Version: 260423-1777562570-887cd48
Browser: Google Chrome
Issue description
Previously, we were able to paste test data directly into table cells using Ctrl+V.
Current behavior
Cells can be edited by typing directly.  
However, pasting using Ctrl+V does not work.  

Could you please confirm whether this is a known issue or a specification change in this version?
If there is a workaround or fix, please let us know.
Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Usage of Data Store and its Impact
entry.content:
Hi Saurav,

Option 2 seems feasible, when data volume is not too large.
Otherwise, JMS offers better performance than Data store, and should be considered instead.

Best regards,
Tom

entry.title: Re: Unable to call Parameterized ODATA services in the bu...
entry.content:
Hi Arjun, 


As per SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002781481, this is limitation of Query Panel in current SAC product, which does not take any URL parameters when acquiring data from S/4HANA.

  • Please treat GRTransactionData and CnsldtnGrpJrnlItem as exception because we support these two services with mandatory parameters.See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/4c002a6b954d43f18f3712cb10ac3cdc.html#sap-s/4hana

In current SAC version,  please take freehand query (see detail from SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002781481） as workaround.


Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Location Master Data Replication: SAP TM to Business Network for Logistics
entry.content:
Location master data is one of the key master data objects in Transportation Management and Freight Collaboration within Business Network for Logistics. Proper location master data management ensures accurate mapping between Business Network for Logistics and Transportation Management systems, enabling seamless transportation processes and freight collaboration

Problem Statement

  • Manual creation limitations: Creating location master data manually in Freight Collaboration is not feasible when dealing with large numbers of location records
  • Maintenance difficulties with large datasets: Manual maintenance becomes difficult and error-prone as the volume of location master records increases
  • Naming convention consistency requirements: Location master codes must be identical in both Freight Collaboration and Transportation Management to ensure proper mapping between systems

Recommended Solution

Implementing the Data Replication Framework (DRF) provides automated replication of location master data from TM to Freight Collaboration, eliminating manual creation challenges and ensuring data consistency across systems

Location is a logical or physical place in which products or resources are managed on a quantity basis.

You use this business object as the basis for transportation processes. In transportation processes, you name a source location, a destination location, and any transshipment locations necessary to complete the transportation process. For this purpose, you define locations.

Tcode - /SCMTMS/LOC3

Manage location app is used to manage location master data, which was manually copied or replicated from SAP S/4HANA or created from new using the app Location master data can be used by other apps, such as Manage Entities, in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration.

We need to create same location master data in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration which is exist in SAP TM means the naming convention must be same.

Hence it is very difficult to create manually location master data one by one. Hence it would be better to replicate the master data automatically in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration.

Master Data Replication in SAP TM - Configuration : 

To set up master data replication, you must configure the replication of master data via the Data Replication Framework (DRF) in the SAP S/4HANA source system. DRF uses SOAP service - LocationBulkReplicationRequest to replicate location master data from the source system to SAP Business Network for Logistics.

Tcode – DRFIMG

Define Custom Settings for Data Replication > Define Technical Settings > Define Technical Settings for Business Systems

Create a business system using your LBN ID

Select the newly created business system and navigate to Define Bus. System, BOs in the dialog structure.

Enter 189 as the BO type and Location as the description.

Enter 189 as the BO type and Location as the description.

Create a new communication channel called Replication via Services

Tcode – DRFIMG

Define Custom Settings for Data Replication > Define Replication Models

Create a replication model.

Select the newly created replication model and choose Assign Outbound Implementation.

Select the outbound implementation 189_1 and choose Assign Target Systems for Repl. Model / Outb. Impl to assign the target system (identified by its LBN ID – as maintained in the business system) to the outbound implementation.

Optional: If you only want to replicate one default language description, select the outbound implementation and navigate to Assign Language

Choose Activate

Tcode – DRFF (Optional Step)

In the worklist, search for the replication model name you have created in step 2, and define the filter criteria for including or excluding business objects

Tcode – SOAMANAGER

Activate consumer proxy: /SCMB/CO_CPX_LOC_BULK_REPL_REQ . Create a logical port and set it as the default port.

TCODE – DRFOUT

Select the newly created replication model and select the following settings under Replication Mode:

  • To send an update of all locations, select Initialization.

  • To send the lasted changed location, select Changes.

  • If you have defined your own filter criteria, select Manual. The manual replication will either be based on the filter criteria defined in step 3, or be based on the filter that you manually maintained in the DRFOUT transaction.

entry.title: Re: SAP EAM Data Profiling in Utilities: What are your proven technical and business rules?
entry.content:
In Utilities, I’ve seen that EAM data only makes sense when you look at it inside the real process. What worked well for us was keeping it simple.
We start with basic checks — missing fields, wrong dates, broken location links. Then we look at where the data actually causes trouble: work orders not scheduling, wrong task lists, or assets showing up in the wrong place.
The last step is event‑based checks. For example, when a meter is installed or moved, we run a quick validation right at that moment. It cuts down a lot of noise and helps teams fix issues before they grow.
This mix of simple rules + process checks + event triggers has been the most reliable approach in my projects.

entry.title: Re: ISU Billing a meter with 3 registers
entry.content:
I’ve seen this exact scenario a few times in Utilities. The meter sends ON‑PEAK, OFF‑PEAK, and a TOTAL register, and the first question is always: “Do I need two rates or one?” The trick is to think about how the customer actually consumes energy, not how the meter stores it.

What usually works well is this:

1. Treat ON‑PEAK and OFF‑PEAK as two separate registers Each register gets its own TOU rate category. You don’t bill the TOTAL register — that one is mostly for validation.

2. Use the time‑of‑use switch to trigger the right rate IS‑U doesn’t guess the time. You define the time slices in the rate and the system automatically picks the right register based on the timestamp of the interval.

3. Update Install Facts with the TOU key Once the TOU key is in the installation facts, the billing engine knows:

  • which register belongs to which time window

  • which rate to apply

  • how to split consumption correctly

After that, billing becomes straightforward. The TOTAL register is only used as a check — if ON + OFF doesn’t match TOTAL, you know the meter has an issue.

This setup keeps things clean and avoids over‑engineering. It’s simple, predictable, and works well for most TOU customers.

entry.title: Re: Need to update Tax jurisdiction code during Contract account change
entry.content:
I’ve had this requirement in a couple of projects, and the pattern is always the same: you need a place where every change to the Contract Account passes through, and you can plug in your own logic before the data is saved.

In IS‑U, the best enhancement for this is event 0050 (FKKVKP: Change Contract Account). This event is triggered every time a Contract Account is created or changed — no matter whether it comes from a user, a program, or an interface.

Inside event 0050, you can read the updated fields, run your tax‑jurisdiction determination logic, and then move the result into FKKVKP‑TXJCD before the save happens.

This keeps the design clean because:

  • You don’t modify standard

  • You catch every update from every source

  • You centralize your tax‑jurisdiction rules in one place

  • You guarantee TXJCD is always filled correctly

If you need to update installation facts later, you can trigger that from the same event or call a follow‑up FM depending on your process.

This approach has worked reliably for us whenever tax jurisdiction needed to be auto‑determined during account changes.

entry.title: CPI logic to ensure that two files are sent together in a single request.
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

In our project for Ariba Remittance, the requirement is to send two interdependent files together as a single bundle. Currently, CPI is sending these files one by one, which is not aligned with Ariba’s expectation. 

we require guidance specifically on the CPI logic to ensure that both files are sent together in a single request. 

Thanks in advance. 

Regards, VS

entry.title: Re: How Will Joule Agents on SAP BTP Transform IS‑U Billing, Exceptions, and Field Operations?
entry.content:
What I’m seeing in Utilities right now is a simple pattern: the biggest pain points are still the same ones we’ve had for 15+ years — billing exceptions, bad meter data, credit issues, and field delays. The difference is that now we finally have the tools to automate the parts that humans should never have been doing manually.

From what I’ve observed across projects, Joule Agents will hit hardest in three places:

1. Billing exceptions and meter data validation This is the area with the most noise. Every utility has thousands of exceptions that repeat every month. Joule Agents can watch the data as it arrives, flag issues early, and even fix the simple ones automatically. This alone can save teams hours every day.

2. Credit & collections Most of the work here is rule‑based: reminders, notices, payment plans, follow‑ups. Joule Agents can take over the repetitive steps and let humans focus on the sensitive cases. It’s a natural fit.

3. Field service scheduling Utilities still struggle with last‑minute changes, no‑access visits, and meter failures. An agent that can look at real‑time events and reshuffle the schedule will make a big difference.

The interesting part is that Joule Agents don’t replace IS‑U. They sit on top of it. They clean the noise, guide the user, and keep the core clean. That’s why I think the biggest long‑term impact will be in clean‑core extensions — letting utilities modernize without touching the old custom code.

We’re entering a phase where Utilities can finally move from “reactive firefighting” to “proactive intelligence.” And that shift is going to change how billing teams, credit teams, and field teams work every single day.

entry.title: I want to change the default order of the code and text for extended fields.
entry.content:
I want to change the default order of the code and text for extended fields.

The image shows journal entry data exported to Excel.

Standard fields (Supplier column) and extended fields (*Customer) are listed side by side.

The display order is reversed between the standard and extended fields: Code (Name) <=> Name (Code).

I want to unify this. Please tell me how to do this (I want the extended fields to follow the standard field order).

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: ATUL Architecture #1: Why Utilities Need an Intelligent Core on SAP BTP Introduction Utilities are
entry.content:
I recently published a blog on why Utilities are moving toward an Intelligent Core on SAP BTP, especially as AMI, billing, and field operations become more event‑driven.

Here’s the blog link for anyone interested in the full breakdown: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-utilities-blog-posts/atul-architecture-1-why-utilities-need-an-intelligent-core-on-sap-btp/ba-p/14387727

The main idea is simple: Utilities are hitting the limits of traditional IS‑U and S/4 customizations. With more data, more exceptions, and more automation needs, the “core” must become lighter — and the intelligence must move to BTP.

I’d love to hear perspectives from the BTP, S/4HANA, and Integration communities:

  • How are you seeing Utilities shift toward clean‑core architecture?

  • Are customers ready for event‑driven patterns and AI‑assisted operations?

  • What challenges do you see when moving intelligence out of the ERP core?

  • Where do you think BTP will create the biggest impact in the next 2–3 years?

Real‑world experiences from architects, consultants, and customers would be extremely valuable. This shift is happening fast, and community insights can help all of us prepare better.

Looking forward to the discussion.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrades: Reducing Complexity and Improving Predictability
entry.content:
Hi Rachid,

Thanks for the great question! Phase remediation of the custom code is the right starting point, since it de-risks the main upgrade project. However, you must also consider the version of your Cloud ERP Private systems. If the systems are in version 2020 or older, they are already out of Mainstream maintenance. Falling out of maintenance means losing access to critical security patches, quality updates, and the latest innovations. To successfully manage the phased approach , you should:

  1. Use tools like the SAP Readiness Check to get a full inventory of your custom code.
  2. Made a specific decision for every impacted object: Remediate, Retire, or Replace.
  3. Prioritized the work, ideally completing the remediation for the most critical code objects first.

Have you looked at RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard yet?  RwSM Dashboard helps you with categorizing the custom code. I suggest reviewing the blog for more details -https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/customer-objects-deep-dive-in-rise-with-sap-methodology-dashboard/ba-p/14317156

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content:
Hi,

Generally, all CDS Views starting from I_* are not direclty exposed via a communication scenario, but used as a data source behing APIs.

 

For example, to extract data from I_JournalEntryItem, you may need to use OData API 

API_JOURNALENTRY_SRV (see help https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/c2e849c5ff5341388dff293e07a4549d.html)
 

On the other hand, you should be able to find all OData API from:

  • OData V2: https://api.sap.com/package/SAPS4HANACloud/odata
  • OData V4: https://api.sap.com/package/SAPS4HANACloud/odatav4 

For example, if you search I_JournalEntryItem, you will get OData V2 API: https://api.sap.com/api/API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV/overview 

 

If you cannot find any API there, please create support case to relevant component.

Like, for all 3 CDS Views you are asking,  you coud create suport case to component FI-FIO-GL-*.

 

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: Access not made

entry.title: Role based security at parent node in SAC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have role based security enabled and want to provide access at the parent level (parent child hierarchy). When we are trying to select Hierarchy and give access at parent node, it does not allow to select the parent node but at flat presentation, it does allow to select the parent but then data cannot be seen in the templates. Role based security does not allow to select the parent node or hierarchy node?

Thanks & Regards,

Pranav Vyas

entry.title: Re: Interest indicators - Tolerance Days
entry.content: Hello Henrike, Even with the link attached, it didnt describe for Due Date+30 days for calculation of Interest. Even it is maintained 30 days as Tolerance, system is picking Due date as Baseline date but not adding 30 days to Due date for calcualtion of Interest

entry.title: Re: Moderation on SAP Community
entry.content: Good information.

entry.title: Mashup Communication between 2 Mashups and parameter PassThrough
entry.content:
Hello community,

having 2 Mashups on BTP (lets say Mashup A and Mashup B),  I want a Button in Mashup A to open Mashup B as a workcenter AND !! parse a custom parameter. 

What is working: Open Mashup B as Workcenter, what is not working: Passing the parameter.

Here is my code from Button of Mashup A:

protected openMashupB(externalId: string) {
const accountMessage = {
operation: 'navigation',
params: {
objectKey: externalId,
routingKey: 'customer.ssc.MashupB',
viewType: 'list',
},
};
window.parent.postMessage(accountMessage, '*');
}
 
I am looking forward to your help !
Regards Stephan

entry.title: Re: Parent-Child Drill-Down Hierarchy from S/4 CDS View t...
entry.content:
Based on my understanding and the behavior I observed, hierarchy (especially drill-down behavior) is only fully supported in SAP Analytics Cloud when using an Import Connection.

In case of Live Connection from S/4HANA CDS views, even though the hierarchy is visible, it does not behave as a proper drill-down hierarchy in SAC.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content: Warnikng message: Connection "S4_JournalEntry_API" is valid, but not all features are available. - Data flows are enabled. - Replication flows are not supported. - Remote tables can’t be used because of errors in the connection. Remote Tables: Unable to connect remote source: DPAdapterAccess::Connect:exception 2520208: SSL requested, but no trust store configured (adapter type: ODataAdapter, source name: DWC_PRD_FINANCIAL_PLAN.S4_JournalEntry_API), Code: 5921, SQL State: HY000

entry.title: Interest calcuation on Customer Open Items with 30/60/90 grace days
entry.content:
Hi,

Business requirement is to calcualte interest from Due date +30/60/90 Grace Days, but system behaviour is that when Tolerance days is configured, if the key date of calculation of interest falls within that Tolerance days, system is not calcuating interest, once the tolerance days got completed, interest is getting calculated from Net Due Date. Please assist.

entry.title: Upcoming Customer Engagement Initiative: AI-Assisted Guidance in UI Theme Designer
entry.content:
With the growing adoption of large language models and conversational AI, it is increasingly important that these technologies effectively understand and support user needs within the UI Theme Designer. This customer engagement initiative explores how SAP Joule and other conversational AI tools can simplify theming workflows, improve usability, and deliver meaningful, context-aware guidance to users.

Together with participants, we aim to:

  • Enhance User Experience
    Enable faster, more intuitive theme creation through AI-driven guidance
  • Evaluate AI Effectiveness
    Assess how well AI understands user intent and delivers accurate, relevant support
  • Improve Documentation Quality
    Identify gaps and enhance clarity, completeness, and accessibility
  • Identify Future Opportunities
    Discover high-impact AI use cases within theming workflows

Key activities will include user scenario testing, hands-on evaluation of SAP Joule and similar tools, user feedback collection, documentation review, and performance analysis. Findings will be translated into concrete, actionable improvements.

Timeline

  • Registration open: until June 21
  • Initial Kick-off Call: early September
  • Deep Dive Sessions: October – November

Registration Link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/4230 

We look forward to your participation and to shaping the future of AI-assisted theming together!

entry.title: Re: 生产工单组件API接口状态字段
entry.content: Could not find CDS view I_MfgOrderComponentWithStatus, Field: MaterialComponentIsDeleted

entry.title: Re: Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
Hi

we installed the SAP CIM on BTP about 2 years ago but stopped evaluating it because it simply could not post even simple invoices.   We now 2 years on want to evaluate it again due to the number of release notes applied to it.  So hoping it will be better.  We have again conducted a very simple test but despite creating an invoice in S4HC it did it without invoice lines despite it matching the PO.   Anyway....   

My question is that there appears to be 2 similar SAP offerings in this space - each with there own Product page   - could you explain the difference and why you might choose one over the other? - SAP Ariba Invoice Management and also SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

entry.title: Re: How to get "DLT" status of production order component
entry.content: Once the order is in the "technically completed" status, the "MatlCompIsMarkedForDeletion" field cannot distinguish whether it was deleted by a person.

entry.title: Crystal Reports runtime application to Crystal Reports 2025 with .NET VB.NET runtime only deployment
entry.content:
We are migrating a large Crystal Reports runtime application to Crystal Reports 2025 (.NET VB.NET runtime only deployment).

Architecture requirements:

  • ~900 reports

  • SQL Server backend

  • runtime-only deployment (no Crystal Designer installed on client machines)

  • reports contain subreports and stored procedures

  • dynamic server/database/user/password passed at runtime

  • reports currently use OLE DB (ADO) with MSOLEDBSQL

  • SQL Authentication

  • CrystalReportViewer preview and PDF export support

Current runtime logic:

  • ReportDocument.Load()

  • DataSourceConnections.SetConnection()

  • Table.ApplyLogOnInfo()

  • subreport table logons also applied recursively

Observed behaviour:

  • tbl.TestConnectivity() succeeds for main report and subreports

  • runtime datasource logging shows updated server/database/user correctly

  • HOWEVER CrystalReportViewer preview and ExportToDisk() still fail with:
    "Database logon failed"

  • VerifyDatabase() also fails

  • Crystal appears to re-open saved datasource metadata internally during render/export

Question:
What is the SAP-recommended architecture for Crystal Reports 2025 when reports must dynamically connect to different SQL Servers/databases at runtime?

Specifically:

  1. Is full runtime datasource identity rebinding officially supported/reliable with OLE DB (ADO) + MSOLEDBSQL?

  2. Is Crystal expected to require a stable saved datasource identity even when runtime logons are applied?

  3. Is the recommended approach:

    • fixed datasource identity + runtime credentials only
      OR

    • full datasource/server/database rebinding?

  4. Are there known Crystal 2025 runtime limitations where:

    • TestConnectivity succeeds

    • but ExportToDisk / CrystalReportViewer render still fails?

  5. Is there a preferred provider/connection method for dynamic runtime rebinding:

    • OLE DB (ADO) + MSOLEDBSQL

    • ODBC

    • native SQL Server driver

    • another supported approach?

entry.title: ewm inbound delivery change item vendor batch and production date
entry.content:
Hi Team , 

I need to BAPI or step to change Inbound delivery Item vendor batch and production date 

i am trying to change the vendor batch and production via /SCWM/RFUI

Please guide

Thanks

entry.title: AI-Assisted Error Resolution for Product Costing Errors
entry.content:
Introduction and Background

Following blog Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur, I would like to introduce how AI can assist you to resolve product cost posting error. The AI-Assisted Error Resolution solution consists of three integrated capabilities that work together to create a seamless error resolution experience.

Situation Handling: Proactively notifies cost accountants when errors occur from event-based production cost postings. This part is covered in detail in a companion blog post by my colleague: "Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur".

Error Explanation: Joule explains to the cost accountant why the error occurred, providing clear root cause analysis.

Step-by-Step Guided Resolution: Joule provides text-based resolution guidance to help the cost accountant resolve the error and proceed with reposting, recommending the best option when multiple resolution paths exist.

End-to-End Process Flow

The process flow can be started in two ways:

Case 1: Starting from a Situation on My Home

  1. A situation is triggered when an event-based order posting encounters an error
  2. The cost accountant receives a situation notification on their SAP Fiori Home page
  3. Clicking on the situation opens the Situation Page with error details
  4. The cost accountant opens Joule and asks for explanation of the error for a specific order
  5. Joule explains the root cause and proposes different resolutions with a recommended option
  6. The cost accountant follows Joule's step-by-step instructions to resolve the error

Case 2: Directly Asking Joule

  1. The cost accountant opens Joule and prompts: "Show me my errors in company code XYZ"
  2. Joule shows the error list based on the user prompt
  3. The user selects one of the items to get an explanation from Joule
  4. Joule explains the root cause and proposes different resolutions with a recommended option
  5. The cost accountant follows Joule's instructions to resolve the error step by step

AI-Assisted Error Resolution — Detailed Solution

In the companion blog "Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur", we covered how Situation Handling proactively notifies cost accountants when event-based posting errors occur. In this section, we dive deeper into the AI-assisted capabilities: how Joule explains errors and guides users through resolution.

Starting from CE2602, the Event-Based Production Costs solution (Scope Item 3F0) supports AI-assisted error resolution through Joule. Once cost accountants are notified of errors via situations, they can leverage Joule to understand the root cause and receive guided resolution steps.

Case 1: Explain Error by Joule (Start from Situation Page)

When a cost accountant receives a situation notification and navigates to the Situation Page, they can see all the affected orders with error details. From there, they can open Joule and ask for an error explanation for a specific order.

How it works:

  1. Navigate to the Situation Page where all affected orders are displayed with error details (message, company code, message class, order number, etc.)
  2. Open Joule and ask for the error explanation for a specific order [ Order number] via a prompt
  3. Joule analyzes the error and provides a clear explanation of the root cause
  4. Joule proposes different resolution options with a recommended option highlighted
  5. Right after the recommended resolution, relevant apps are proposed for proceeding to the next step (e.g., "Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing", "Schedule Product Costing Jobs")

Figure 1: Situation Page with Joule providing error explanation and resolution options

Figure 2: Joule recommends the best resolution option and proposes relevant apps for reposting

As shown above, Joule not only explains the error but also provides actionable resolution steps with a clear recommendation. The cost accountant can then directly open the suggested app to proceed with reposting.

Case 2: Joule Shows Error List Based on User Prompt

If cost accountants want to know about existing errors in their company code directly — without navigating through an active situation — they can simply ask Joule.

How it works:

  1. Open Joule and ask: "Show me errors in company code [XXXX]" (company code must be specified)
  2. Joule shows the error list based on the user prompt; by default it displays 6 items with a direct "Explain Error" button for each
  3. The user can click "Explain Error" on any item to get Joule's root cause analysis and resolution guidance
  4. The user can also click "View More" to see additional errors in Joule, or "Open in App" to navigate to the Manage Product Costing Errors app for full error management

Figure 3: Joule displays the error list with "Explain Error" buttons and the Manage Product Costing Errors app in the background

Figure 4: Error list in Joule showing orders with errors, each with an "Explain Error" action button

Case 3: Joule Shows Situation List Based on User Prompt

For cost accountants who want to get an overview of all their active situations, they can ask Joule to show their situation list directly.

How it works:

  1. Open Joule and prompt: "Show me my situations"
  2. Joule shows the situation list with details including description, status, and age
  3. Each situation has a "Show Details" button for deeper investigation
  4. The user can also click on the "My Situations – Extended" app link to view the full situation overview in the dedicated app

Figure 5: Joule displays the situation list with "Show Details" buttons

Figure 6: My Situations – Extended app with Joule side panel showing situation details

Additional Information

Limitations

  • The explanation result supports English language only with release CE2602 delivery. This limitation will be removed in a later release.
  • In release CE2602, there is no direct integration between situation and Joule — the cost accountant needs to open Joule separately and ask for the error explanation. This limitation will be removed in a later release.

Useful Links

  • SAP Help Portal – Production Accounting
  • Video in discovery center: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, error resolution for cost accounting

Related Blog

This blog is part of a two-part series on AI-assisted error handling for product costing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud: "Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur" — Covers situation handling setup and notifications.

entry.title: Re: Building Agentic Hybrid RAG with Knowledge Graphs + Vectors - Developer Guide
entry.content:
Thanks for the great article—really insightful!
I had a quick question: I understand the advantages of Hybrid RAG, but I was wondering if in many cases it might be sufficient to rely primarily on a Knowledge Graph, and have the agent consult documents only when needed.

When do you think Vector RAG is still necessary or especially useful in practice?
And do you see any benefits in combining this with a Property Graph approach?

If you have any concrete examples or decision criteria, I’d love to hear them!

entry.title: Re: No Successor for FOR FIORI APP id F3556 F3557
entry.content:
Hi,

It seems there will be not successors for that kind of apps.

Please find note: 2869647-Guidance for use of Data Aging in SAP S/4HANA

Best regards,

Bruno

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content:
Dear @ranguduvv_prasad,

for our Integrated Financial Planning (Content for xP&A) we provide the several APIs to import master and transaction data into SAC. You can find a full list in our help docu, APIs for Financial Planning and Analysis.

Within our business content we offer OPEX, CAPEX, and Financial Statement planning with pre-configured import jobs for your reference. Please find all details in the SAP Help portal, Financial Planning in SAP Analytics Cloud.

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,
Susanne

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start - Rebranding of the app, enhanced Joule and improved single sign-on functionali
entry.content:
Dear SAP Product Experts!

To go ahead is fine, more or less, it depends on the effort. 

Does somebody had in mind that a company would like to communicate such changes before the these changes become to reality?

Could you imagine how many support calls will be generated by such changes?

<ironic on> It is realy very agile </ironic off> to react by surprise. Please communicate such basic changes like names and design some weeks earlier to enable the support and the administrators to present these information to the end user.

Thank you very much for an stressfull monday morning!

Kind regards!

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content: Hi, I cannot see any attachment. Where are you trying to create Generic Data Connection and what service URL are you using. From the error you are facing (Data flows are enabled. - Replication flows are not supported. - Remote tables can’t be used because of errors in the connection), it seems that it is not connection in SAC.

entry.title: Re: Ariba Invoice : Total Amount not getting added correctly
entry.content: Hi Kausika, Supplier did not maintain correct Tax jurisdiction code in their system that was causing the issue

entry.title: Re: Establish Connection Between SAC and On-Premise HANA DB
entry.content:
Hi,

 

If you are using Live Tunnel connection to SAP HANA which leverage SAP Cloud Connector to transfer the HTTPS request from SAC to SAP HANA, please ensure that you have configured SAP Cloud Connector correctly:

- You have configured the subaccount of SAC tenant in Cloud Connector

- You have configured system mapping and accessible resource correctly

Refer to:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/3bdb65253c8046b2b8234c554072569f.html

Before using SSO authentication, it is strongly recommended to establish Live Tunnel connection using basic auth firstly.


Best regareds,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: No Successor for FOR FIORI APP id F3556 F3557
entry.content:
Hello,

Looks like the BC-CCM-DAG team already shared information about your concern.

Please read the note 2869647-Guidance for use of Data Aging in SAP S/4HANA.
As described in the note, for application data, we find that customers’ intended access to data is very often incompatible with the performance characteristics of aged data.
Section Further Technical Considerations in this note, talks about the other methodologies that can be used instead of data aging.

There will be no further developments in data aging. We are providing the support to our existing customer for data aging.

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: Re: To-Do option in FIORI, but under the "My Home Pag...
entry.content:
Hello customer

This could be missing from the user authorization

  • TASKPROCESSING  (namespace /IWPGW/) for My Inbox
  • C_SITNMYSITUATION_CDS - for My Situations (Standard only supported for now)
  • INTEROP -  available with the basic role required to run the launchpad ex SAP_FLP_USER or classical SAP_UI2_USER_700 ).
  • /UI2/INSIGHTS_CARDS_READ_SRV (Service Group /UI2/INSIGHTS_SRV)

Here is the documentation related to My Home page section

My Home in SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAC reports to live HANA prompting for user name and...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I believe that you are using Live Direct connection to SAP HANA using basic authentication, which is designed to prompt credential everytime.

If you want to avoid such credential prompt, you need to enable single sign-on (SSO)  for the live connection with the following Prerequisites met:

  • If you want users to have a single sign-on experience to your data, check that you are using the same Identity Provider (IdP) for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP HANA. For more information on setting up your identity provider in SAP Analytics Cloud, see Enable a Custom SAML Identity Provider (Legacy Custom IdP).

  • Ensure that all users are SAML configured.

  • Ensure that the InA package (/sap/bc/ina/service/v2) or a higher-level package is configured for SAML authentication. For details, see the SAP HANA XS Classic Configuration Parameters.

 

Refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/58c890e1c89d41e69b2cec31bac2d95f.html 

 

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content: Hi, i am not able to attach the screenshot. I have created odata generic conneciton and with url but receiving the warning message as mentioned before. Issue is not able to view the required JOURNALENTRYITEM API in the connection in Datasphere Dataflows.

entry.title: Asset transfer within the co code depreciation issue
entry.content:
• Formwork has been purchased on multiple dates.
  • There have been several transfers made within the profit centres based on requirements
  • Depreciation is being calculated on a weighted average basis based on purchase date of each individual lot
  • Due to these transfers, the depreciation calculation is impacting individual projects depreciation expense.
  • However, the overall depreciation percentage remains consistent.
  • Kindly advise a suitable mechanism to  be carried out to rectify the depreciation discrepancies. Using the T code ABUMN

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to have multiple SAP cloud connections...
entry.content:
Hi, 

 

Yes, you can add same SAC subaccount into multiple instances of SAP Cloud Connector but differ using Location ID (ensure that the Location ID in Cloud Connector should be also configured or used in SAC)

 

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content: Hi Susanne, I dont see any data extraction enabled CDS Views or Queries to extract data in s4hana public cloud. Please proivde list of cds views which can be utilized for data extraction. I have already gone thru all these portal blogs but no cds view is found which is extraction enabled for public cloud

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - External logging to Datadog Using OpenTelemetry
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) with Software version update 8.42.* has introduced support for OpenTelemetry (OTEL) as an external logging destination, allowing you to stream Message Processing Logs (MPL) directly to OTEL-supported observability platfroms like Datadog, Grafana, Splunk etc for centralized observability.

What is OpenTelemetry? OpenTelemetry is a vendor-neutral framework for collecting, processing, and exporting telemetry data—metrics, logs, and traces. It's widely adopted across the industry and supported by most observability platforms, including Datadog. This standardized approach means your integration logs can be correlated with other systems and applications in your Datadog environment.

In this guide, I will walk through the step-by-step configuration needed to send CPI logs to Datadog using the OTEL integration, including prerequisites, BTP setup, and validation of the data flow.

Prerequisites

Before you begin, ensure you have the following:

  • An active Datadog account with access to the organization settings
  • A Datadog API key (available at Organization Settings > API Keys)
  • BTP subaccount with Connectivity and Destination access
  • Acess in BTP subaccount to create/update service instance of Process Integration Runtime with appropriate roles

Step 1: Configure Your Datadog Setup

First, prepare your Datadog environment to receive logs from CPI.

Get Your API Key

Navigate to your Datadog organization settings and create an API key if you don't have one already. Go to Organization Settings and select API Keys. Create a new key and copy it—you'll need this for the BTP destination configuration.



Identify Your Datadog Region and OTEL Endpoint

Depending on where your Datadog organization is hosted, you'll use a different endpoint. For example:

  • EU region: https://otlp.datadoghq.eu/v1/logs
  • US region: https://otlp.datadoghq.com/v1/logs

Confirm your organization's base URL (it appears in your browser when you log in) and append the OTEL log endpoint accordingly.

Step 2: Create the BTP Destination

Now configure the HTTP destination in SAP BTP that will serve as the bridge between CPI and Datadog.

Navigate to Destinations

Log into SAP BTP, open your integration subaccount, and navigate to Connectivity > Destinations.

Create a New Destination

Click "Create Destination" and configure it with the following parameters:

Name: CloudIntegration_MonitoringDataConsumer
Type: HTTP
Authentication: No Authentication
URL: https://otlp.datadoghq.eu/v1/logs

Authentication set to "No Authentication"?  Yes—the Datadog API key will be passed as a header, not as basic auth.

Add Additional Properties

Scroll down and add the following additional properties. These fields tell Datadog how to classify and organize your CPI logs:

Property Name Value Purpose

OTELServiceNameMessageProcessingLogsIdentifies the service sending logs in Datadog
OTELScopeNameMPLLogical grouping identifier for CPI logs
OTELScopeVersion1.0.0Version of the log schema
URL.headers.dd-api-key<Your Datadog API Key>Authentication header for Datadog ingestion
TargetSystemOTELSpecifies OpenTelemetry as the target system

Important: Replace <Your Datadog API Key> with the actual API key you created earlier. Store this key securely and never commit it to version control.

After entering all properties, click Save.

Step 3: Activate External Logging in CPI

With the destination configured, you now need to activate external logging at the tenant level.

Create a Service Instance

In your BTP subaccount, go to Services > Instances and Subscriptions. Create a new instance of Process Integration Runtime with the following settings:

  • Service: Process Integration Runtime
  • Plan: api
  • Roles: ExternalLoggingActivate, ExternalLoggingActivationRead

Once created, generate a service key and copy the following credentials:

  • Client ID
  • Client Secret
  • Token URL
  • API Host (base URL for your CPI tenant)

Authenticate and Call the Activation API

Use Postman (or curl) to authenticate with the token URL and then call the external logging activation endpoint. Here's the flow:

1. Get an OAuth token:

POST {TokenURL}
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials
&client_id={ClientID}
&client_secret={ClientSecret}

Copy the access_token from the response.

2. Activate external logging:

POST https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/activateExternalLogging?defaultLogLevel=INFO
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}

The defaultLogLevel parameter controls which log levels are sent. Options are:

  • NONE—disable external logging
  • INFO—send info, warn, and error logs (recommended)
  • ERROR—send error logs only

A successful response confirms external logging is now active. You should see logs flowing to Datadog within a few seconds.

Verify Activation Status

To confirm external logging is active, query the status endpoint:

GET https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/ExternalLoggingActivationStatus('{TenantName}')
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}

A successful response will show the activation status as true.

Step 4: Verify Data Flow and View Logs in Datadog

Once external logging is activated, CPI will begin sending Message Processing Logs to Datadog in real-time.

Querying Logs in Datadog

In Datadog, open the Logs section and search for logs with the service you configured in BTP Destination OTELServiceName.

service:MessageProcessingLogs

You should see MPL entries streaming into Datadog logs.

Parsing MPL Content as JSON

CPI sends MPL logs in a structured format, but the log content itself may be nested. To extract individual MPL attributes and create reports or dashboards, you can use Datadog's Pipelines feature:

Option 1: JSON Parser — If your log body is valid JSON, use the JSON parser to flatten the structure and extract fields. 

Option 2: GROK Parser — For more complex log formats, use a GROK parser pattern to extract specific fields.

Note: My Datadog was missing JSON parser, so I created 2 GROK Parsing rule as below after carfully verifing all the log content format ingested by SAP CI for various combination (ApplicationMessageId, SenderId, CustomStatus, LastError, LastErrorModelStepId etc) to parse all MPL logs.


Once parsed, you can facet logs by integration name, message ID, status, and other attributes to build dashboards, set up alerts, and troubleshoot issues faster.

Maintenance and Troubleshooting

Updating the Destination

If you need to modify the destination (e.g., change the API key or region), follow this sequence:

  1. Call the deactivateExternalLogging endpoint
  2. Update the destination in BTP
  3. Call the activateExternalLogging endpoint again

Skipping deactivation can result in not reflect the destinatinn updates.

Known Limitations

When CPI sends logs via OTEL, the timeUnixNano and observedTimeUnixNano fields are currently set to 0 instead of a proper Unix epoch value for custom headers. Datadog doesn't process these malformed timestamps, so log ingestion still works but only for Run Events and not Custom Headers. I believe SAP will address this in a future update.

Useful API Endpoints

For reference, here are other relevant endpoints for managing external logging:

Deactivate external logging:
POST https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/deactivateExternalLogging

View external logging events:
GET https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/ExternalLoggingEvents

Check MPL logs (alternative query):
GET https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs

For detailed API specifications, refer to the SAP API Hub documentation on External Logging.

entry.title: Re: 100558 - Map Format Data for Payments: User-Defined F...
entry.content:
Hi Teddy, The 'structured addresses' feature has been released. It simplified the data mapping in context with address related information in the payment medium. Here is the help document for your reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/a214adb709d740fea55ed1ee83b0994f.html?version=2508.500

Below is a screenshot of tag '':

 

Thanks. 

Hongliang

entry.title: Re: May 2026 Developer Challenge — Week 2: Connecting to the broker and consuming events
entry.content: Very nice solution. Thanks for the thorough explanation!

entry.title: Re: May 2026 Developer Challenge — Week 2: Connecting to the broker and consuming events
entry.content: Remember to "persist" the messages so you don't end up losing events in case the consumer becomes unavailable.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content: Hi Xiwen, Problem i am facing is while creating odata generic connection in Datasphere with service url getting the warning message. Connection is valid and also data flows are enabled. But i dont see the JOURNALENTRYITEM_SRV API in the data flows when i select the connection. Let me know what am i missing.

entry.title: Re: Considerations when implementing IAS for SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
@taylor_mcdonald71 

 

I would still challenge your HR and onboarding teams with the following?

Why are they not completing the ONB process? Is it too long cumbersome? If they are not completing the process then are they skipping any compliance aspects, what does your compliance and legal team think about this?  If they do not complete the process how do they get the correct data for EC?  This is pushing work on HR & IT?  I would also be really clear to hires that we need their details so we can get them set up and ready to pay them!  You really need to push back on this, the challenges you are facing are not in IT but in poor recruitment and ONB governance!

There is one thing that may help.  Your design principle seems that you replicate on their hire date which is good.  I would add in another filter and say ‘Hiring is not complete’ flag which mean they will only go when there is a complete EC record. As a result the ONB record will not replicate on their hire date.  What this would mean is if they have not finished the process they can log in and finish their ONB process off on their first couple of days.  This would solve lots of your IAS issue caused by poor Recruitment and ONB governance.

entry.title: Re: Power BI connectivity to SAP
entry.content:
Hi  Team,

I do NOT see any attachments and what is the error message you are getting .

Please follow below steps to connect Power BI to HANA:

  1. Install Latest HDB client software in system where you want to connect Hana Database. Hana client software enables the ODBC Driver as below. 
  2. Type ODBC in windows search bar and click ODBC data source 32/64 bit.
  3. Click add on Database.
  4. Double click on HDBODBC.
  5. Enter below details and save the connection.
    Host: Hana database host
    Instance number as example 00
    And tenant database would be Hana system name
  6. Go to power BI and go to Hana database connection.
  7. Enter username and password of SAP Hana Database 


Refer the below Knowledge Base Articles : 

3623376 - How to connect Power BI to HANA DB

2882465 - When using Power BI to connect to HANA DB using ODBC DSN, an authentication error is generated

Hope this is helpful.

 

~Gaurav

entry.title: Next-Level Cash Forecasting and Planning with SAP BDC and SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Still forecasting cash with disconnected spreadsheets?  There’s a better way.

Join our upcoming SAP Partner session: “Next-Level Cash Forecasting and Planning with SAP BDC and SAP S/4HANA”.

We’ll dive into how organizations are:

  • Connecting treasury and operational data
  • Improving liquidity visibility across planning horizons
  • Using AI to support smarter forecasting decisions
  • Moving toward scalable, automated CFP processes

Plus, we’ll discuss how profitability insights and cash forecasting work better together when data silos disappear.

One missed forecast can impact on investments, hiring decisions, or supply chain planning.  That’s why modern CFP matters more than ever.

Seats are filling quickly.  Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014163.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPFinance #LiquidityPlanning #DigitalTransformation #EnterprisePlanning

entry.title: Re: sql anywhere 17 documentation
entry.content:
Well, the official SAP SQL Anywhere 17 help is not located on the former Sybase documentation pages but on SAP Help...😉:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SQL_Anywhere

Besides the 17.0.0/17.0.1 help, there are also links to the documentation sets for former versions, albeit just as PDF bundles.

entry.title: Charts / KPI Visualization with RAP Custom Entities
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a RAP application using custom entities, where both entities are implemented through the same ABAP class. The association between these entities is working as expected, and I have successfully handled filtering and paging as well.

At the moment, I can display the List Report, Object Page, and Line Item Reference navigation without any issues.

However, I would now like to enhance the main page by adding visualizations such as charts or KPI-style data representations based on the aggregated data.

I have explored several resources, but I haven’t been able to find a clear or reliable approach for achieving chart/KPI visualization directly using custom entities in RAP.

Is there any supported way or recommended approach to implement such visualizations in this scenario?

Any guidance or suggestions would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to hide personal data during hiring manager review
entry.content: Hi this is during the Recruitment process when you require the hiring manager to review the applications.  So the RCM module

entry.title: Re: Information Error "Follow-up action EWM_AP not al...
entry.content:
Dear Dhrumit J Shah,

thank you for the details provided.

Please read following KBA in the topic:

3580360 - Error QV211 in QA11.

I wish you a nice day,
​Albert
​***
​Read and follow the 'SAP Community Rules of Engagement' at
​https://www.sap.com/community/about/rules-of-engagement.html

entry.title: Re: Custom Logic to Implement Flexible Billing Document Numbering
entry.content:
Hey

Very useful blog! I did the same for the Distribution Channel, created a custom field and use the data transfer to fill it. 

Important note: After publishing the Badi, you still have to perform 2 customizing steps:

1) Define custom routines for data transfer to Billing Documents (as described)

2) Important to define the copying control!! For example, if you define the routine for the copy from debit memo request to debit note, you must use the config: Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Billing Document, for DR to L2, item category L2N.

Once you maintained that, it will work.

entry.title: Re: How to pre-create the packages for the ABAP Flight Reference Scenario
entry.content:
I used this and pulled successfully with a small fix at Line 168.

Add this to line 168

( package_name = '/DMO/FLIGHT_REUSE_DATA_GEN' super_package_name = '/DMO/FLIGHT_REUSE' short_description = 'Flight Reference Scenario: Reused Data Gen'
    )

entry.title: What's Hybrid Specification Management?
entry.content:
With the May release, SAP IPD Specification Management is now integrated with SAP S/4HANA Specification Management with a hybrid solution, where customers can store all their specification data in their SAP S/4HANA system, but still use the features of SAP IPD, for example, but not limited to: 

  • Supplier collaboration within the system utilizing the SAP Ariba integration
  • Comparing specifications for informed decisions
  • Easily find specifications with AI support

Here is a video overview of the functionality:

 

More Information

You can find a more detailed description on the Help Portal: Hybrid Specification Management | SAP Help Portal and its subpages.

If you are interested in what needs to be configured before using the scenario, see: Integrating with SAP S/4HANA Specification Management | SAP Help Portal and its subpages.

What's Next?

Stay tuned for a more detailed overview on the configuration tasks that need to be done before the functionality can be used.

About the Author

Zsofia is a development manager at SAP IPD, working in the PLM area for 10+ years.

entry.title: Re: Closing Journal Entries Are Missing in Generated e-Le...
entry.content:
Dear Elena,

Yes, the configurations you mentioned are already maintained correctly on our side. The SAP document type ZK is mapped to the relevant GIB external document type in the e-Ledger configuration. However, in the e-Ledger execution screen, there is no separate parameter available for selecting special periods. I

n the “Run Statutory Reports” app, only Company Code, Reporting Month, and File Type can be selected. If the issue is related to the inclusion of special/closing periods, could you please clarify where and how this special period selection should be maintained or controlled in the system?

Also, as this is a productive system and due to the GIB legal deadline, I have already opened an SAP support case regarding this issue.

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit: Stuck in "preparation" for migrati...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can check the running activity in SE16 and filter by project or object.

/LTB/MC_EVENTLOG  long running activity Direct Transfer
/LTB/JOB_QUEUE  long running activity Excel
/LTB/MC_TASKPROC  long running activity Staging

entry.title: Cancellation of billing invoice even it is referenced to create credit memo
entry.content: User has created billing invoice, then created credit memo with reference to billing invoice after that system allows to cancel billing invoice without cancelling credit memo & note. This is the SAP standard behaviour but how we can stop this process that if any invoice has been referenced to create credit memo then system will not allow to cancel billing invoice until & unless all the document get reversed.

entry.title: How can SAP solutions help automate business processes?
entry.content: How can SAP software help businesses automate their daily work and operations?

entry.title: Re: EWM material type check before inventory creation
entry.content: Thank you, but i have several customers with sever release very recent ad i hace checked all systems. Very strange.

entry.title: Re: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q2 2026
entry.content: Hi @Orla_C , any idea if this Asymmetric Reporting in SAC story will be made available for Weekly based Date dimensions?

entry.title: New SAP Data Architecture Learning Journey & SAP Certified Data Architect Certification is now Live
entry.content:
TLDR: The Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Learning Journey is now live on learning.sap and alongside it the New Role Play based SAP Certified Data Architect certification is now available in this learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-a-certified-sap-data-architect

Early in my career, I sat in a room full of architects debating a major platform decision. The conversation covered integration patterns, application landscapes, and total cost of ownership. No one discussed the data. Not the quality of it, not how it would flow, not how it would be governed. We treated data as the responsibility of someone else (master data team and BW team) in a different meeting. Years later, we are seeing AI initiatives slow down because of untrustworthy data, I understand exactly what was missing from that room.

Data architecture has spent years as a supporting discipline important, but rarely the headline. That has changed. The shift to agentic AI has moved data architecture from a back-office concern to a front-line competitive capability.

Agentic AI systems do not simply retrieve information. They reason, plan, and act. They require data that is real-time, contextually grounded, semantically rich, and governed with precision. An agent operating on stale, fragmented, or poorly modelled data does not just produce mediocre outputs it can produce incorrect actions. At enterprise scale, that is a governance and business risk problem, not just a technical one.

The architects who will lead in this environment are those who can hold both conversations at once: the strategic (how data drives business value) and the technical (how to design the platforms, integration patterns, and governance structures that make that value real).

That is exactly what we have tried to address with the New Data Architecture learning Course (Advanced) and a New Data Architect Certification from SAP

Introducing the Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Course

 

The Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Learning Journey (course BDCDA2) is now live on learning.sap.com. Some of you might have done ‘Becoming a Data Architect – Foundational Course’ which was launched last year and this new course takes a deeper look at the topics related to Data Architecture.

Across four-unit journey designed for architects who are ready to move beyond foundational concepts and into the architecture decisions, framework synthesis, and platform-specific depth that define effective data leadership in the agentic AI era.

  1.  Unit 1: Architecting Data Strategy for the Agentic AI Era

The journey opens with strategy, because that is where most data initiatives fail first. Unit 1 builds the five foundational pillars of data strategy: Governance, Data literacy, Stewardship, Architecture, and Technology enablement and connects each directly to AI readiness. It examines what agentic AI demands from data pipelines that traditional analytics architectures were not built to provide: real-time streaming, semantic enrichment, and contextual grounding. It then moves to leadership and culture, the organizational factors that determine whether a data strategy survives contact with reality and closes with a framework for measuring data strategy success and analyzing real-world value patterns.

  1. Unit 2: Integrating Frameworks for Data Architecture

Architecture without a framework produces inconsistency. Unit 2 examines the three frameworks that define the professional standard in this field: TOGAF, DAMA-DMBOK, and the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework and, guides how to synthesize them rather than apply them in isolation. It then applies that synthesis to two of the most significant architectural paradigms in the market today: Data Mesh and Data Fabric.

  1.  Unit 3: Harmonizing SAP Business Data Cloud Within the Open Data Ecosystem

Unit 3 is where strategy and frameworks meet the SAP platform. It begins with BDC Connect and the Delta Sharing protocol the mechanism that enables zero-copy data sharing across ecosystem boundaries and then works through each major integration scenario in the open data ecosystem: Databricks, Snowflake (including all three connectivity options), Microsoft Fabric, and Google Big Query. The unit closes with a dedicated lesson on data federation performance optimization the practical trade-offs between federation, replication, and zero-copy sharing that every architect advising on SAP Business Data Cloud deployments needs to understand cold.

  1. Unit 4: Designing Advanced Data Models and Architecture for SAP Business Data Cloud

The final unit addresses SAP Business Data Cloud as a Business Data Fabric how it implements federation, replication, and zero-copy sharing to connect heterogeneous data sources while preserving business context and semantic coherence. It covers Data Mesh in SAP BDC, including how Datasphere Spaces implement the four Data Mesh principles in practice. It then moves to one of the most consequential topics in enterprise AI architecture: semantic models and knowledge graphs as the foundation for trustworthy AI and RAG-based systems.

 Introducing the SAP Certified Data Architect Certification

Alongside the learning journey, we have launched the SAP Certified Data Architect certification.

The SAP Certified Data Architect is an AI role-play-based certification that assesses how well candidates understand modern data architecture in the context of strategic business success. It covers the five major architectural paradigms: Data Warehouse, Data Lake, Data Mesh, Data Fabric, and Data Lakehouse, alongside the core elements of data maturity: governance frameworks, data security, and business-aligned data strategy. It uses interactive question-and-answer format, assessing the understanding of SAP Data Architecture in the agentic AI era specifically the ability to synthesize and articulate how SAP Business Data Cloud enables governed, semantically coherent data access across hybrid cloud environments.

Note: It is recommended that the candidate completes the 1) Becoming an SAP Data Architect and 2) Mastering SAP Data Architecture courses before attempting the certificate to ensure successful completion.

 Who This Is For

The learning journey and certification are designed for practitioners who are already working in the data and architecture space and want to develop the rigor, depth, and formal recognition to operate at senior level. That includes data architects designing enterprise data platforms, enterprise architects expanding their data architecture competency, analytics and BI leaders responsible for governed data access, and technical decision-makers advising customers on SAP Business Data Cloud strategy.

 If you are starting your data architecture journey, the foundational 'Becoming a Data Architect' learning journey on learning.sap.com is the right starting point. The advanced journey picks up where that one ends.

Getting Started

  1. The Becoming a Certified SAP Data Architect Learning Journey live now for both internal and external learners: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-a-certified-sap-data-architect
  2. SAP Certified Data Architect certification registration: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-data-architect-sap-business-data-cloud

A Final Thought

In the agentic AI era, the constraint on enterprise intelligence is rarely the algorithm. It is the architecture underneath it the decisions made about how data is structured, governed, connected, and made meaningful. Those decisions require a specific set of skills. This learning journey and certification exist to build them.

The practitioners who invest in that depth now will be the ones designing the data foundations that AI-enabled enterprises run on. That work is not infrastructure. It is strategy.

Happy Learning!

entry.title: Worker Concept in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I'm researching Authorization in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have several questions about Workers.

 1. What is the definition of a Worker in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? Why is a Worker required in the system?

 2. What is the process for creating a Worker in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

 3. How can a Worker be removed from the system? If the Worker cannot be removed, what are the reasons?

Thank you for your answer.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Error in “Assign FS Item Mappings” Tile – “The from fiscal year period must be between 01 and 000”
entry.content:
When trying to create a new entry in the “Assign FS Item Mappings” tile in FIori , the following error message appears:

“The from fiscal year period must be between 01 and 000.”

Could you please let me know the cause of this error and how to resolve it?

entry.title: Re: Not able to generate DVM report for BW4HANA system in...
entry.content: In SAP BW/4HANA you can use the Data Volume Statistics.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core ( Coimbatore, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, June 17th, 2026 from 10:00 - 15:30 IST in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address:  Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited, CHIL-SEZ, Keeranatham Village, Coimbatore - 641 035, Tamil Nadu.

The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @sheenamk

The event is kindly hosted by Bosch Coimbatore. Thank you!

Prerequisites: 

Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Vivaldi, Brave, Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A confirmation email will be send to all registered participants 1 week before the event.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Role based security at parent node in SAC
entry.content:
Indeed, no hierarchies available in role security.
Suggest you to vote on  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/297401 and/or https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/259971 
You can try using properties instead as workaround.

entry.title: Re: Document Migration: Understanding User Difference Between Onboarding 1.0 and BizX
entry.content:
Thank you for confirmation @JacsonMiranda!

Is there any confirmation on the postponed date as in how much more time we have?

Please let us know.

Thank You
Chetan Paradkar

entry.title: Re: Building Custom Joule Capabilities: Getting Started
entry.content: Thanks for that, I fully understood this after a 3rd read of the documentation! Thanks for the link, definitely a new bookmark page.

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP: ES Plastic Tax - Missing Tax ID for delivieries in EU Acquisition Book
entry.content: This topic was addressed via a Service Now ticket. The decribed case will be covered by in a future release. Deliveries from non-EU plants to Spanish customers will be displayed with the plant name and tax number of the delivery plant.

Have you followed the steps I sent earlier regarding authorization in SAP Help portal?

After that CDS should be available along with the field

Best Regards,

Dawid
